dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: There are three ways to survive the apocalypse. Everything is fading and becoming unclear now, but one thing is certain. If you're reading this, you will survive. The End.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: [THREE WAYS TO SURVIVE THE APOCALYPSE FINAL EPISODE]

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: There are three ways to survive the apocalypse. Everything is fading and becoming unclear now, but one thing is certain. If you're reading this, you will survive. This is the last episode.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Really? Is that it?

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: RATTLE

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Episode 1 Prologue: Three Ways to Survive the Apocalypse.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: RATTLE

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: I'm Dokja.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: RATTLE

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Reader, or individualist in Korean. Dokja can mean only child. This is how I introduce myself to others.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: And nine times out of ten, people misunderstand me.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Oh, so you're an only child?

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Well, yes, but that's not what I meant.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Oh... Then what did you mean?

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: It is my name.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Dokja Kim. Employee Dokja. My father gave me that name because he wanted me to grow up to be a strong and independent person.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: But perhaps my name is a curse... because I've been lonely and single my entire life.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Oh, I see.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: This is who I am in a nutshell.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Dokja Kim (28). Graduated from a third-rate college and currently employed as a contractor at a subsidiary of a large corporation.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: My hobby is reading serialized web novels on the subway on my way home from work.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: But today's subway ride was unlike any other.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Oh, my. At this rate, you might get sucked into your phone.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: Step

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Sangah Yu from HR?!

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: H-hello.

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: Are you heading home?

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Yes, you too?

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: Yes. I got lucky. My team manager is away on a business trip.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: Flinch

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: POOF

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: I can't believe I ran into Sangah, the most popular girl at work. Her shampoo smells really nice.

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: Do you usually take the subway home?

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: From the HR assistant manager to the finance team manager, I'll bet there's a line of men waiting to drive her home...

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: Ha. Actually, someone stole my bicycle.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: You commute to work on a bike?

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: Yes. I've been working a lot of overtime lately, so I thought I could use the exercise. And I have some personal reasons too.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Seeing her up close, I get why all the guys are head over heels for her. Well, none of that concerns me. She and I live completely different lives.

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: Haha.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: And just so you know, Sangah wasn't the reason why today's subway ride was different.

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: ¿Puede prestarme dinero?

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Pardon?

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: It's Spanish. It means "Please lend me some money."

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Studying on the subway... She's a different breed altogether. Where would she even use that phrase?

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: You're studying hard. What about you, Dokja? What are you reading?

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm... "THE WORLD AFTER APOCALYPSE CHAPTER 1: THE SKY BECAME DARK BY THE GIGANTIC TOWERS HIDDEN IN THE SHADOWS OF THE ULLSHED EARTH. BUILDINGS SCREAMING AND THE SCATTERED MOANING SOUNDS COULD BE HEARD. AND THEN THERE WAS SILENCE."

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: Is it a novel?

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Yeah... I guess you could say that. I'm studying Korean. Haha.

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: Wow! I love novels! My favorite authors are Murakami Haruki, Raymond Carver, Han Kang, and... of course.

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: Who's your favorite author, Dokja?

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: You probably wouldn't know even if I told you. Haha. I can never tell her what I'm really reading. That's right.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: My hobby is reading serialized web novels on the subway on my way home from work.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Although I'm not proud of my hobby, the reason today is unlike any other is...

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: "The World After The Apocalypse" Author: Singshong, 169 Episodes. "Three Ways to Survive the Apocalypse" Author: TLS123, 49 Episodes.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Because "Three Ways to Survive the Apocalypse," or TWSA for short, which I've been reading for the past ten years, is ending today.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: People might wonder why I'm making such a fuss over the ending of a web novel, but this story in particular is very special to me.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: I've been reading this series since I was in eighth grade. I read it when I was bullied at school.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: When I failed my entrance exams and ended up at a third-rate college.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: When bad luck got me stationed on the front line during my military service.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: And even now, as an unstable contract worker getting transferred from company to company.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Damn it. What's the point? Anyway... I don't have a single happy memory.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: It's bittersweet that this fictitious world I've been following for so many years was now coming to an end.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Today is the day the epilogue of TWSA will be uploaded.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: A grand serial consisting of 3,149 episodes. A ten-year-long epic journey that began in my late teens and ended in my adulthood.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: I can't believe the series went on for so long with just an average view count of one.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The first chapter had around 1,200 views, but by chapter ten, there were only about 20 views. Then the view count dropped to 12 by chapter 50. And now, only one, I'm the one reading.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: It was a story written for me and only me.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: I wanted to support the author with a donation.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: KIM DOKJA: I'LL BE LOOKING FORWARD TO THE EPILOGUE. THANK YOU FOR EVERYTHING, SIR.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: But it was hard enough for me to even make ends meet, so I just thanked the author by writing a comment.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: I tried recommending the series to others, but they just criticized me.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: People would either claim it's a great series if you can bear through it, or call me an attention-seeker. People can be so cruel.

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: Dokja?

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: Oh! Come to think of it, it has already been a year since we started working at the company. We started around this time last year. Time sure flies by, doesn't it?

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: I forgot she was here.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: I should change the topic.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Yeah, we had no idea what we were getting into back then. Haha.

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: Yeah... It seems like it was only yesterday. I can't believe our contracts are ending soon. Haha.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Oh. I forgot. Sangah was promoted to full-time employee last month due to her diligence.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Oh, I... Ah, that's right. Sorry for the late congratulations. Haha.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: I should've learned a foreign language too.

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: No, it is fine, Dokja. I haven't completed my performance assessment yet, and...

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: I don't want to admit it, but Sangah is so cool. If we were characters in a novel, she would definitely be the heroine. It was obvious.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: I didn't try, but Sangah gave it her all.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: I read web novels while Sangah studied, so it was obvious that Sangah would become a full-time employee and I wouldn't.

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: Um... Dokja?

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Yes?

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: If you want... I could tell you the name of the app I use...

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: If I were in a different genre... If the genre of my life wasn't non-fiction... But fantasy: Could I have been the protagonist?

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: Dokja?

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: RATTLE

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: RAT-TLE

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: It's okay, Sangah.

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: Huh?

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: I don't know. If one thing was certain... it wouldn't matter if you told me the name of the app or not. It is that the genre of my life is non-fiction.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: And in this genre, I'm no protagonist. I'm merely a "reader." I have to live my own life.

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: Your own life...?

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: You're so inspiring, Dokja.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Sorry?

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: I should also live my own life!

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: ?! Huh?

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: Like you, I'll strive to live my life to the fullest! Let's do this!

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Well... at least that got her to stop talking...

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: BZzT

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: A message?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Back then, I had no idea.

dialogue.speaker: TLS123
dialogue.line: THANK YOU.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: POP

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: TLS123? Huh? Is this the author of TWSA?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: What would happen to me next?

dialogue.speaker: TLS123
dialogue.line: THANK YOU.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Are you the author of TWSA?

dialogue.speaker: TLS123
dialogue.line: Thanks to you, I was able to finish my story. I also won a prize in a contest.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: Pop

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: POP

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: What? TWSA was in a contest? It really is him!

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Congratulations! But what kind of contest?

dialogue.speaker: TLS123
dialogue.line: It's not a big contest, so you wouldn't have heard of it.

dialogue.speaker: TLS123
dialogue.line: Oh, and the series will become paid content soon.

dialogue.speaker: TLS123
dialogue.line: Starting with the epilogue today, TWSA will become paid content.

dialogue.speaker: TLS123
dialogue.line: As a token of my gratitude, I would like to send you a special present. Could you tell me your email address?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: What was about to happen to this world?

dialogue.speaker: TLS123
dialogue.line: After all, I was able to publish my story thanks to you.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Present?

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Wow. TWSA won a contest? An unknown contest? I guess he doesn't want to tell me because he's embarrassed.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: RACE

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: A present? Is it a gift card? It would be great if it's a $50 gift card.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: You have one new message.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: I-it came! The present!

dialogue.speaker: TLS123
dialogue.line: THANK YOU FOR EVERYTHING. SENDER: tls123 <123@naver.com> 6:55 PM. MR DOKJA KIM, STARTING AT 7 PM TODAY, TWSA WILL BECOME PAID CONTENT. THIS WILL HELP YOU. I WISH YOU ALL THE BEST.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Starting at 7 PM? It is 6:55 right now, so that means I have five minutes left. I'll go into my favorites and...

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Huh?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: No results were found.

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: That is weird. Do they remove serials without prior notice when they become paid content?

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Hmm? What...

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: AHHH

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: W-what is happening?!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Is it a terrorist attack?

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: AHHH

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: pat

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: PANT

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Sangah, are you okay?

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: Ah, what is happening? Y-yes. What do you think is happening?

dialogue.speaker: Dokja
dialogue.line: Don't worry. It is probably nothing--

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: It was by sheer coincidence that I checked the time at that exact moment.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: It is 7 PM, so the paid content...

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Ladies and gentlemen, your attention please.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: P-please, evacuate. Evacuate.

dialogue.speaker: Sangah
dialogue.line: Dokja! What is going on...?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: ATTENTION. THE FREE SERVICE OF THE 8612TH PLANETARY SYSTEM HAS COME TO AN END. THE MAIN SCENARIO WILL BEGIN NOW.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: CAh

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: It was at that moment that the genre of my life changed.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: CAACA

summary: Dokja Kim, an ordinary contract worker, reflects on his life and his decade-long habit of reading the web novel "Three Ways to Survive the Apocalypse" (TWSA), which is ending today. He encounters his popular colleague, Sangah, on the subway, and they discuss their lives and hobbies. As Dokja anticipates the final episode of TWSA, he receives a message from the author, TLS123, announcing the series will become paid content and offering a special gift. However, at 7 PM, the world suddenly changes with a system message declaring the end of "free service" and the start of the "main scenario."
